attribs.name: npc_condition_immunities
attribs.current: blinded, charmed, deafened, exhaustion, frightened, incapacitated, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, stunned, unconscious

attribs.name: repeating_npctrait_-MjJFwpRBt2EcrbG92a3_description
attribs.current:
Creature Capacity: 30 crew, 20 passengers
Cargo Capacity: 100 tons
Travel Pace: 5 miles per hour (120 miles per day)

attribs.name: repeating_npctrait_-MjJKivsBvWAheoUqOQ8_description
attribs.current: Repairs to a damaged ship can be made while the vessel is berthed. Repairing 1 hit point of damage requires 1 day and costs 20 gp for materials and labor.

attribs.name: repeating_npctrait_-MjivI1NAioeesDwOqNi_description
attribs.current: A hanging lantern or two casts bright light across the ship.

attribs.name: repeating_npctrait_-MjivM2nRLbdJQL4VkWc_description
attribs.current: Rigging on the ship can be climbed without an ability check.

attribs.name: repeating_npctrait_-MjivWIYv0o6b3HgL6G4_description
attribs.current: The footlockers are iron and have AC 19, 18 hit points, and immunity to poison and psychic damage.

attribs.name: repeating_npctrait_-MjivkYJG57oIiKX2bVb_description
attribs.current: The ship is steered by a rudder control on the aft of the deck. The Rudder is a control surface that turns the ship.

attribs.name: repeating_npctrait_-MjivpV1XAwPl2raHtqc_description
attribs.current: The longship has one 20-foot-tall mast with sails that can be used to sail the ship.

attribs.name: repeating_npctrait_-MjivvGP0VLHg0N5ZCIU_description
attribs.current: Sixteen benches are built into the deck, each with a 15-foot-long oar. When the ship is rowed, crew members sit on these benches to work the oars. Five spare oars hang on the walls.

attribs.name: repeating_npctrait_-Mjiw0L5iLWbHNsnsExl_description
attribs.current: The deck has a 3-foot-high rail covered in wooden shields around its perimeter that provides half cover for Medium creatures and three-quarters cover for Small creatures behind it.

attribs.name: repeating_npctrait_-MjiwEXL5p7Ep-mIyUu2_description
attribs.current: The ceilings in the lower deck, holds, and cabins are 8 feet high with 6-foot-high doorways.

attribs.name: repeating_npctrait_-MjiwHyyUZLGEhcC3n7c_description
attribs.current: The ship's doors are made of wood and have AC 15, 18 hit points, and immunity to poison and psychic damage. A lock can be picked with a successful DC 15 Dexterity check made using thieves' tools, or the door can be forced open with a successful DC 20 Strength (Athletics) check.

attribs.name: repeating_npctrait_-MjiwP0ZAg26p1ZHSDKk_description
attribs.current: A covered, 10-foot-square opening leads to the lower deck.

attribs.name: repeating_npctrait_-MjiwV0OBI5ndT9Uk2vb_description
attribs.current: Four rowboats are stacked on top of each other on this deck. Ropes and pulleys can hoist these boats in and out of the water.

attribs.name: repeating_npctrait_-Mjiwb7VdXnyHv3bLAXJ_description
attribs.current: Benches line the walls of this room. Four holes carved in them house chamber pots.

attribs.name: repeating_npctrait_-MjiwwYL9JQcWcmSs40w_description
attribs.current: This cabin is meant for guests and visiting high-ranking officials along for the journey. Since warships often have a greater need to house prisoners rather than guests, each bed aboard such a vessel also has a set of manacles (see chapter 5 in the Player's Handbook) attached to its frame.

attribs.name: repeating_npctrait_-MjixLMZOoUK8Vqv2moS_description
attribs.current: Cargo holds at the fore and aft of the lower deck hold crates, barrels, and ammunition secured with rope.

attribs.name: repeating_npctrait_-MjixPdGGOMhrOsjfGxj_description
attribs.current: The ship has ports in its lower decks that can be opened to fire cannons.

attribs.name: repeating_npctrait_-MjixeAq9HaNkJwAbnoA_description
attribs.current: The ship's wheel stands at the aft of the deck. This is used to steer the ship. If destroyed the ship can not take the turn actions. (Unless the rudder or other control surface is manipulated manually.)
